title: Marry Me Chicken
tag: Creamy chicken skillet with sun-dried tomatoes
ingredients: Pasta, rice, or mashed potatoes for serving

subsections.title: Chicken Seasoning
items: A pinch of red pepper flakes

directions: Pat the chicken breasts dry and season heavily on both sides. Let sit for 10-15 minutes while the pan heats.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken for 4-5 minutes per side until a deep golden crust forms. Remove and set aside.
Lower heat to medium. Add butter and onions, then cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Add spinach here if using.
Stir in the sun-dried tomatoes and tomato paste. Cook for 1-2 minutes to deepen the flavor.
Pour in chicken broth and scrape up the browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Reduce heat to low. Stir in heavy cream and Parmesan until smooth and creamy.
Return chicken to the skillet. Spoon sauce over the top, cover, and simmer for 8-10 minutes, or until chicken reaches 165 F internally.
Stir in spinach until wilted. Taste and adjust salt, pepper, or red pepper flakes.
Serve over pasta, mashed potatoes, or rice. Garnish with fresh basil and extra Parmesan.
upgrade: Before adding the cream, splash in about 1/4 cup of the oil from the sun-dried tomato jar. It adds a rich restaurant-style flavor.

title: Sweet Heat Salmon, White Rice & Broccoli
tag: Glazed salmon with jasmine rice and tender-crisp broccoli

subsections.title: Sweet Heat Sauce
items: Hot honey, or regular honey plus red pepper flakes

directions: Rinse 2 cups jasmine rice until the water runs mostly clear. Add to a pot with 3 cups water and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let sit covered for 10 minutes before fluffing.
Season both sides of the salmon generously with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, Cajun seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
Heat olive oil and a little butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook salmon about 4-5 minutes per side, depending on thickness. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, combine honey, sweet chili sauce, soy sauce, garlic, sriracha if using, and lemon or lime juice. Simmer for 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Return salmon to the pan. Spoon sauce over each fillet and let it glaze for 1-2 minutes.
Steam or saute broccoli until tender-crisp. Season lightly with butter, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Plate jasmine rice first, add broccoli on the side, top with sweet heat salmon, and drizzle extra sauce over the rice.

title: Rasta Pasta
tag: Creamy jerk chicken pasta with peppers
ingredients: Red, yellow, and green bell peppers
directions: Season chicken generously with Walker'swood Mild Jerk Seasoning, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, and black pepper. Marinate for 20-30 minutes if possible.
Boil bow tie pasta according to package directions. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken until golden and fully cooked. Remove and slice into strips or bite-sized pieces.
In the same pan, add butter. Saute onions and peppers until slightly softened but still vibrant. Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add 1 cup chicken broth and scrape up the flavorful bits. Stir in 1 1/2-2 cups heavy cream and 1/2 cup Parmesan. Simmer until sauce begins to thicken.
Stir in 1-2 tbsp Walker'swood Mild Jerk Seasoning, adjusting to taste.
Add cooked pasta and chicken back to the skillet. Toss until coated. Add reserved pasta water if the sauce is too thick.
Garnish with extra Parmesan and black pepper. Serve hot.
upgrade: Add 2-4 oz cream cheese when making the sauce. It makes the pasta extra creamy and helps the jerk flavor cling to every bite.

title: Garlic Butter Steak Alfredo
tag: Steak strips tossed with linguine in garlic butter Alfredo
directions: Season steak strips generously with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, black pepper, salt, and Italian seasoning.
Boil linguine according to package directions. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear steak strips until browned and cooked to your liking. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add 4-5 tbsp butter to the same skillet. Add 5-6 cloves minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in 1 cup chicken broth and scrape up the browned bits. Add 2 cups heavy cream and simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Stir in 1-1 1/2 cups grated Parmesan until smooth and creamy.
Add linguine to the sauce and toss. Add reserved pasta water if needed to loosen.
Return steak to the skillet and toss everything together. Garnish with parsley and extra Parmesan.
upgrade: After removing the steak, add 1 tbsp steakhouse seasoning and a splash of Worcestershire sauce to the garlic butter before adding cream.

title: Chicken Nachos
tag: Loaded sheet-pan nachos with salsa chicken and queso
ingredients: Shredded Mexican cheese blend or cheddar-jack blend
directions: Dice chicken into small bite-sized pieces. Season generously with mild taco seasoning.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken until browned and mostly cooked through. Add red salsa and stir to coat. Simmer 3-5 minutes until thickened and fully cooked.
Spread tortilla chips evenly on a sheet pan or oven-safe platter. Add shredded cheese, salsa chicken, and another light layer of cheese.
Bake at 375 F for 5-8 minutes, or until cheese is melted.
Top with warm white queso drizzle, pico de gallo, jalapenos, and sour cream.
Serve immediately while chips are warm and crispy.
upgrade: Warm the queso separately and drizzle it on after baking. It stays smooth, creamy, and gives a loaded sports-bar finish.

title: Mediterranean Bowl, CAVA Style
tag: Harissa honey chicken bowl with cilantro lime rice

subsections.title: Seasonings
items: Chicken: garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, black pepper, Italian seasoning optional
Broccoli and corn: olive oil, garlic powder, salt, black pepper

directions: Cook jasmine rice according to package directions. Fluff and mix in lime juice, chopped cilantro, a little butter if using, and salt to taste.
Season chicken with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook in a skillet until browned and cooked through.
In the last few minutes, add 2 tbsp harissa paste and 2 tbsp honey. Toss until coated and slightly caramelized. Slice or dice.
Toss chopped broccoli with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Roast at 425 F for 15-20 minutes until charred around the edges.
Roast or saute corn until lightly charred. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Combine shredded lettuce and cabbage blend in a large bowl.
Layer cilantro lime rice, lettuce and cabbage, harissa honey chicken, roasted corn, charred broccoli, and feta cheese.
Drizzle with Brianna's Caesar Style Dressing and add an extra squeeze of lime if desired.

title: Ground Turkey & Charred Sweet Potato
tag: Taco-salsa turkey with buttery cinnamon sweet potato
directions: Preheat oven to 425 F. Wash and dry sweet potatoes. Lightly coat with olive oil and roast for 45-60 minutes, depending on size, until fork tender.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey and break it apart as it cooks.
Once mostly browned, add mild taco seasoning and stir well. Pour in red mild salsa and simmer 3-5 minutes until thickened.
Slice each sweet potato down the center. Gently mash the inside with 1 tbsp butter, cinnamon, and a drizzle of maple syrup if using.
Fill each sweet potato with taco-salsa ground turkey and optional toppings. You can also eat the turkey and sweet potato separately.

title: Jerk Chicken Caesar Salad
tag: Charred jerk chicken over chopped Caesar salad
directions: Season chicken breast generously with Walker'swood Mild Jerk Seasoning and a drizzle of olive oil. Marinate 20-30 minutes if possible.
Heat a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat. Cook chicken until nicely charred and fully cooked through.
Let chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice or dice into bite-sized pieces.
Wash and finely chop romaine lettuce. Place in a large bowl.
Add Brianna's Caesar Style Dressing, Parmesan cheese, and freshly ground black pepper. Toss until evenly coated.
Crush croutons slightly and sprinkle throughout the salad.
Top with jerk chicken, extra Parmesan, and black pepper.

subsections_after.title: Marie Stems Meal Prep Ratio Per Bowl

title: Chicken Alfredo Flatbread Pizza
tag: Flatbread topped with spinach Alfredo, chicken, and mozzarella
ingredients: Flatbread, naan, artisan flatbread, or pizza flatbread
directions: Season chicken with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
Sear in a hot skillet with olive oil until golden and fully cooked. Let rest, then slice or dice.
Melt 2 tbsp butter in a skillet. Add 3-4 cloves minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in 1 cup heavy cream and simmer 2-3 minutes. Stir in 1/2-3/4 cup Parmesan until smooth. Season lightly with black pepper.
Stir spinach into Alfredo sauce until wilted. Remove from heat.
Place flatbread on a baking sheet. Spread spinach Alfredo over the flatbread. Add chicken, mozzarella, and extra Parmesan if desired.
Bake at 400 F for 10-12 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly and edges are crisp.
Cool 2-3 minutes, slice, and serve.

title: BBQ Chicken Flatbread Pizza
tag: BBQ chicken, mozzarella, red onion, and optional cilantro
ingredients: Flatbread, naan, artisan flatbread, or pizza flatbread
directions: Season chicken with garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, black pepper, and salt.
Sear in a hot skillet with olive oil until golden and fully cooked. Let rest, then dice.
Toss cooked chicken with a few spoonfuls of BBQ sauce until evenly coated.
Place flatbread on a baking sheet. Spread a thin layer of BBQ sauce across it.
Add a light layer of mozzarella, BBQ chicken, thinly sliced red onions, and more mozzarella.
Bake at 400 F for 10-12 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly and edges are crisp.
Drizzle with extra BBQ sauce, sprinkle with cilantro if desired, slice, and serve.

title: Fruity Pebble French Toast, Turkey Bacon & Cheesy Scrambled Eggs
tag: Colorful sweet brunch plate with savory sides
ingredients: Thick-cut brioche bread or Texas toast
Shredded cheese, cheddar, Colby jack, or Mexican blend
directions: Crush Fruity Pebbles into small pieces and place in a shallow dish.
Whisk together 4 eggs, 1/2 cup milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, and 1/2 tsp cinnamon.
Dip each slice of brioche into the egg mixture, then press both sides into crushed Fruity Pebbles.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Cook each slice 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Cook turkey bacon in a skillet, oven, or air fryer until crispy.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Melt butter in a nonstick skillet over low-medium heat and slowly scramble. Add shredded cheese near the end and fold until creamy.
Stack French toast, dust with powdered sugar, drizzle with maple syrup, and serve with turkey bacon and cheesy eggs.

title: French Toast Breakfast Sandwich
tag: Sweet-and-savory French toast sandwich with turkey bacon and eggs
ingredients: Thick-cut brioche bread or Texas toast
directions: Whisk together 4 eggs, 1/2 cup milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, and 1/2 tsp cinnamon.
Dip each slice of brioche into the mixture and cook in a buttered skillet over medium heat until golden on both sides.
Cook turkey bacon until crispy.
Scramble eggs or make folded eggs for easier sandwich assembly. Season lightly with salt and pepper. Add cheese during the last minute of cooking.
Layer French toast, cheesy eggs, turkey bacon, optional extra cheese, and a second French toast slice.
Drizzle lightly with maple syrup, dust with powdered sugar if desired, slice, and serve.
upgrade: Brush the outside of the French toast with melted butter and maple syrup before assembling for a sweet-and-savory brunch finish.

subsections_after.title: For 4 Sandwiches
items: 4 slices American cheese or 1 cup shredded cheese

title: Cinnamon Toast Crunch French Toast Bites
tag: Crispy cinnamon cereal-coated bites with cinnamon drizzle
ingredients: Thick-cut brioche bread or Texas toast
directions: Cut brioche into bite-sized cubes.
Whisk together 4 eggs, 1/2 cup milk, 1 tsp vanilla extract, and 1/2 tsp cinnamon.
Crush Cinnamon Toast Crunch into small pieces and place in a shallow bowl.
Dip bread cubes into the egg mixture, then roll each piece in crushed cereal until coated.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Cook bites 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crispy. Work in batches.
For the drizzle, whisk 1 cup powdered sugar, 2-3 tbsp heavy cream or milk, 1/2 tsp cinnamon, and 1/2 tsp vanilla until smooth and pourable.
Place bites in a serving bowl or tray, drizzle with cinnamon glaze, and dust lightly with powdered sugar.
upgrade: Add a splash of brown sugar creamer to the drizzle for a cinnamon-roll flavor.

subsections_after.title: Serving Note
items: These come out like little cinnamon-toast donut bites with a sweet glaze on top. They work well for brunch boards, pop-up breakfasts, or party trays.
